perturbation.original_text: Distributor agrees not to represent or sell other products which are deemed to be competitive with the Company's Products unless agreed to by the Company by written notice.
perturbation.changed_text: Distributor agrees not to represent or sell other products which are deemed to be competitive with the Company's Products, including but not limited to any other nasal aspirators or devices designed for similar purposes, without explicit written consent from the Company. For the avoidance of doubt, the Distributor may freely represent and sell products that, while serving related markets, do not directly compete with the Snotarator™ Nasal Aspirator.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity regarding what constitutes a 'competitive' product. The original text provides a general restriction. The altered text specifies some items as competitive while leaving others vague, creating uncertainty and potential disputes. This contradicts the later sections defining the Distributor's rights and responsibilities, specifically regarding exclusive distribution as another sections implies. The phrase 'For the avoidance of doubt, the Distributor may freely represent and sell products that, while serving related markets, do not directly compete with the Snotarator™ Nasal Aspirator' creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company hereby appoints and grants Distributor the exclusive right to sell the products of the Company, including the Snotarator™ Nasal Aspirator, ("Products") listed in the current "Price List" (Exhibit "A" attached hereto). The distribution right shall be limited to all countries and all of their territories and possessions within the continent known as South America ("Territory") (Exhibit "B" attached hereto). Distributor agrees that the Products, including the name Snotarator ™ Nasal Aspirator (the "Brand") are the sole property of the Company and Distributor has no interest whatsoever in such Brand and Products, and Distributor shall use the Brand and Products only for so long as the rights granted hereby remains in full force and effect. Distributor shall not take any actions, or aid or assist any other party to take any actions that would infringe upon, harm or contest the proprietary rights of Company in and to the Brand and Products. Distributor shall not assign the rights granted in this Section 1.01 without the prior written consent of the Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided a general restriction on representing or selling competitive products. The changed text attempts to clarify this by specifying that 'competitive' includes certain items (nasal aspirators) but then introduces an exception: the Distributor can sell products in 'related markets' that don't 'directly compete' with the Snotarator™. This creates an in-text contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text,' which grants the Distributor the 'exclusive right' to sell the Company's products, including the Snotarator™, within a specific territory. By allowing the Distributor to sell 'related' products that don't 'directly compete,' the changed text undermines the exclusivity granted in the 'Contradicted Text,' as the Distributor could potentially sell similar products, blurring the lines of exclusivity and creating a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: Company agrees, at its own expense, to indemnify, defend and hold harmless each Distributor and its customers from and against every expense, damage, cost and loss (including attorneys' fees incurred) and to satisfy all judgments and decrees resulting from a claim, suit or proceeding insofar as it is based upon an allegation that any Product or any part thereof furnished by Company or any process which is practiced in the customary use of the Product is or has been infringing upon any patent, copyright or proprietary right, if Company is notified promptly of such claim in writing and given authority, and full and proper information and assistance (at Company's expense) for the defense of same.
perturbation.changed_text: Company agrees to undertake reasonable efforts, at its own discretion and expense, to assist Distributor in defending against claims alleging that any Product infringes upon any patent, copyright or proprietary right. However, the Company's total liability for such claims, including all expenses and attorneys' fees, shall not exceed $10,000. The Company's decision to provide legal defense or settle any claim shall be at its sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: This changes the nature of the Company's obligation from 'indemnify, defend and hold harmless' to 'undertake reasonable efforts to assist,' capping liability. This introduces contradiction and uncertainty. The original text implies the company will cover all cost, while the changed one is giving a fixed ammount, and only to 'assist'. This contradicts the section that talks about warranty from the product in Article V. In text contradictions, because on one hand the company provides a warranty, but restricts any legal liabilities due to possible infrengment. This introduces an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company warrants that Distributor shall acquire Products purchased hereunder free and clear of all liens and encumbrances except for Company's purchase money security interest defined in Section 1.03, above. Company further warrants all Products to be free from defects in material or workmanship under normal use and service for a period of ninety (90) days from the date of delivery. Any defects must be replaced by the Company within sixty (60) days within this scope of the warranty and all charges for labor and material, will be borne by Company. If it is determined that either no fault exists in Company, or the damage was caused by negligence of Distributor, its agents, employees or customers, Distributor agrees to pay all charges associated with each such replacement. THIS CONSTITUTES THE SOLE WARRANTY MADE BY COMPANY EITHER EXPRESSED OR IMPLIED. THERE ARE NO OTHER WARRANTIES EXPRESSED OR IMPLIED WHICH EXTEND BEYOND THE FACE HEREOF, HEREIN, INCLUDING THE IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. IN NO EVENT SHALL COMPANY BE LIABLE FOR ANY INCIDENTAL OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES AND DISTRIBUTOR'S REMEDIES SHALL BE LIMITED TO REPAIR OR REPLACEMENT OF NONCONFORMING UNITS OR PARTS. 2
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction because it limits the company's liability to $10,000 and changes the obligation to 'assist' in defending against claims, while the contradicted text, under 'warranty', states that 'all charges for labor and material will be borne by Company' if defects are present. This creates an in-text contradiction because the company limits liabilities. This introduces ambiguity and potential for dispute, fulfilling the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement may be extended for an additional two year period with the written consent of both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement may be extended automatically for successive one-year periods, unless either party provides written notice of non-renewal at least ninety (90) days prior to the end of the then-current term. The Company retains the sole discretion to terminate the agreement at any time during the extension periods with a 30-day notice.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces a contradiction regarding the extension of the agreement. Originally, it required mutual written consent for a two-year extension. The modified version allows for automatic one-year extensions unless there is a notice of non-renewal, and also the company now have unilateral power to terminate on extension. This contradicts the termination cause which is listed under the '6.02. Termination' where it explicitly states By the written consent of both parties, to terminate the agreement. This introduces an in-text contradiction, by having two ways of terminating a deal when the contract explicitly says there is only one way of doing it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may be terminated only: (a) By either party for substantial breach of any material provision of this Agreement by the other, provided due notice has been given to the other of the alleged breach and such other party has not cured the breach within (30) days] thereof; or (b) By the Company if: there is an unacceptable change in the control or management of the Distributor; if the Distributor makes an assignment for the benefit of creditors; if a petition in bankruptcy is filed by or against the Distributor, resulting in an adjudication of bankruptcy; or, if the Distributor fails to pay its debts as they become due and provided due notice has been given by the Company to the Distributor and the Distributor has not cured such breach within thirty (30) days thereof; or (c) By the written consent of both parties; (d) Upon termination of this Agreement all further rights and obligations of the parties shall cease, except that Distributor shall not be relieved of (i) its obligation to pay any monies due, or to become due, as of or after the date of termination, and (ii) any other obligation set forth in this Agreement which is to take effect after the date of termination.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a new way to terminate the agreement (Company's unilateral termination during extension periods with 30-day notice), which directly contradicts the original termination clause that explicitly states termination can only occur under specific conditions, including 'By the written consent of both parties'. This creates an in-text contradiction as per the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition because it introduces inconsistent obligations regarding termination. A reasonable reader would see that either parties would need to agree to terminate, when the modified text grants the company the right to terminate at any time.
